steps.title: Identify the logical relationship
steps.content: The passage says critics compliment the novel “despite the damage ______ Soviet censors.” Soviet censors caused the damage to the novel. The blank must express that the censors were the agent (source) of the damage, not the recipient of it.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — bestowed on: Means given as a gift or honor to someone. Violates logical direction — this makes the censors the recipients of a gift, not the cause of damage. ✗
  • Choice B — wrought by: Means caused or produced by an agent. “The damage wrought by Soviet censors” correctly identifies censors as the cause. ✓
  • Choice C — allocated to: Means officially assigned or distributed to a recipient. Violates logical direction — damage is not distributed to censors; censors inflict it. ✗
  • Choice D — concealed from: Means hidden away from someone. Violates the passage — the damage is not hidden from censors; censors are the ones who caused it. ✗
B is correct.
